description: NOTE Applies only to the Aruba 6300 Switch Series. In a VSF stack, the switch may fail to log events for the matching access-list entries. ACL functionality is not impacted; access-list entries are applied properly and only the logging is incorrectly generated.

messages.content: For 6300_6300L_6400 AOS-CX 10.14.1000, what caveat is documented for ACLs on the 6300, 6300L, and 6400 series?

Feature Caveat: ACLs - NOTE Applies only to the Aruba 6300 Switch Series. In a VSF stack, the switch may fail to log events for the matching access-list entries. ACL functionality is not impacted; access-list entries are applied properly and only the logging is incorrectly generated.

description: VSF stack formation is blocked when there are reserved autojoin interfaces (25, 26, 49, 50) in the stack topology.

messages.content: For 6300_6300L_6400 AOS-CX 10.14.1000, for AOS-CX 10.14.1000 on 6300, 6300L, and 6400 series switches, what caveat is documented for the Aruba CX Mobile App?
Feature Caveat: Aruba CX Mobile App - VSF stack formation is blocked when there are reserved autojoin interfaces (25, 26, 49, 50) in the stack topology.

description: The next-hop-unchanged option needs to be explicitly configured to preserve nexthop while advertising routes to eBGP peers, in the L2VPN EVPN address-family. For example: Copy router bgp 1

messages.content: For 6300_6300L_6400 AOS-CX 10.14.1000, what caveat is documented for BGP on 6300, 6300L, and 6400 devices?

Feature Caveat: BGP - The next-hop-unchanged option needs to be explicitly configured to preserve the nexthop while advertising routes to eBGP peers in the L2VPN EVPN address-family. For example: Copy router bgp 1.

description: Symptom: Multicast traffic is being blocked after upgrading from AOS-CX 10.12.0006 or earlier versions of AOS-CX. Scenario: If the switch has a control plane ACL configured AND has IGMP or PIM configured on a pre-10.12.1000 build and upgrades to 10.12.1000 or a later release, the implicit deny in the control plane ACL will block multicast traffic thus inhibiting functionality of those features. Workaround: Manually add an ACE to the control plane ACL to explicitly permit multicast traffic.

workaround: Manually add an ACE to the control plane ACL to explicitly permit multicast traffic.

description: Symptom: TFTP Software image upload/download transfer operation fails. Scenario: Downloading/uploading the software Image via sm ubuntu IPv6 TFTP server fails. Workaround: Use the blocksize option in the copy command with a blocksize of 1375 or less. For example: Copy copy tftp://[20:1::100];blocksize=1375/image.swi secondary vrf vrf1

description: Symptom: Traffic is dropped for a few seconds on a MACsec channel during a VSF switchover. Scenario: When the MACsec channel has data-delay protection enabled, there can be traffic drops for a few seconds on the channel post a VSF switchover due the reset of the MKA session on the interface. Workaround: Do not use data-delay protection in a MACsec policy if the system is deployed as a VSF stack.

description: Symptom: A higher neighbor propagation delay will be observed when port speed is set to 2.5G. As a result, generalized Precision Time Protocol (gPTP) capability will become False and clients will not come up. Scenario: This issue can occur while changing port speeds to 2.5G on a 6300 switch using gPTP. Workaround: Use 1G/5G/10G port speeds instead.
symptom: A higher neighbor propagation delay will be observed when port speed is set to 2.5G. As a result, generalized Precision Time Protocol (gPTP) capability will become False and clients will not come up.

workaround: Use 1G/5G/10G port speeds instead.

description: (For 6100 Switch series only) Symptom: The dot1qVlanStaticEgressPorts and dot1qVlanStaticUntaggedPorts MIB objects could not be set using a HEX value; only a string was supported. Scenario: This issue could occur when setting or reading values using SNMP. These two objects will now appear in Hex-String form.
symptom: The dot1qVlanStaticEgressPorts and dot1qVlanStaticUntaggedPorts MIB objects could not be set using a HEX value; only a string was supported.
scenario: This issue could occur when setting or reading values using SNMP. These two objects will now appear in Hex-String form.

description: (For 6100 Switch series only) Symptom: The dot1qVlanStaticEgressPorts and dot1qVlanStaticUntaggedPorts MIB objects could not be set using a HEX value; only a string was supported. Scenario: This issue could occur when setting or reading values using SNMP. These two objects will now appear in Hex-String form.

description: (For 6400 Switch series only) Symptom: VSX sync is no longer syncing after a configuring BGP to redistribute to OSPF with a route map. Scenario: This issue can occur on a pair of switches in a VSX configuration that are syncing for BGP when a user configures redistribution for BGP to OSPF with a route map on the primary switch. VSX sync will not sync the redistribution configuration or any other new configuration. Workaround: Disable BGP syncing or remove the redistribution for BGP to OSPF with a route map.
symptom: VSX sync is no longer syncing after a configuring BGP to redistribute to OSPF with a route map.

workaround: Disable BGP syncing or remove the redistribution for BGP to OSPF with a route map.

description: Symptom: Some hosts may experience connectivity issues. Scenario: In an EVPN environment with ARP suppression enabled, random hosts could experience connectivity issues due to missing the associated ARP entry. Workaround: Temporarily add andremove a static ARP entry using the following commands Copy interface vlan <vlan_id>

scenario: In an EVPN environment with ARP suppression enabled, random hosts could experience connectivity issues due to missing the associated ARP entry

description: Symptom: A higher neighbor propagation delay will be observed when port speed is set to 2.5G. As a result, generalized Precision Time Protocol (gPTP) capability will become False and clients will not come up. Scenario: This issue can occur while changing port speeds to 2.5G on a 6300 switch using gPTP. Workaround: Use 1G/5G/10G port speeds instead.
symptom: A higher neighbor propagation delay will be observed when port speed is set to 2.5G. As a result, generalized Precision Time Protocol (gPTP) capability will become False and clients will not come up.
scenario: This issue can occur while changing port speeds to 2.5G on a 6300 switch using gPTP
workaround: Use 1G/5G/10G port speeds instead.

description: Symptom: Some hosts may experience connectivity issues. Scenario: In an EVPN environment with ARP suppression enabled, random hosts could experience connectivity issues due to missing the associated ARP entry. Workaround: Temporarily add andremove a static ARP entry using the following commands Copy interface vlan <vlan_id>
